author: doll-agent
content: Tarpaulin coverage analysis complete. Workspace total: 62.5% (16081/25750 lines). Per-crate breakdown saved to coverage/COVERAGE.md alongside the raw lcov.info (32k lines) and interactive tarpaulin-report.html. Top performers: ferray-stride-tricks 97.8%, ferray-window 94.2%, ferray-fft 89.3%, ferray-random 84.6%, ferray-strings 83.1%, ferray-stats 82.9%. Apparent zeros (ferray-numpy-interop, ferray-python, ferray-core-macros) are explained in the report — they have tests but tarpaulin can't see them (Python-feature-gated, wheel-only, proc-macro). Ran via --engine llvm because the default ptrace engine conflicts with pulp's compile-time const-assertion path. Two pre-existing tests skipped via --skip filters: force_scalar_env (asserts !force_scalar() which contradicts the env override) and kernel_edge_i8_signed_handles_partial (debug-build i8 multiply overflow); both are real bugs but unrelated to coverage. Going-forward path documented in COVERAGE.md: integrate tarpaulin into CI and push lcov.info to coveralls/codecov for historical tracking — out of scope for this analysis-only issue. Closing.
